game_name: World of Illusion Starring Mickey Mouse and Donald Duck

element_introduction_order.element: Cape attack (flourishing capes to defeat enemies)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary combat mechanic taught immediately alongside movement

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy defeat transformations (doves, cards, harmless objects)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Visual feedback reinforcement for combat success
Defeating boss unlocks new magic spell for progression
New spell required to traverse next level's unique challenges
Spell-gated locomotion mechanic changing level traversal
Environmental hazard mitigation via spell power

element_introduction_order.element: Character-specific route branching (Mickey gaps, Donald alternatives)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Level design accommodates character differences, creates path variation

element_introduction_order.element: Co-op mechanics (shoulder stacking, rope assists, pulling through gaps)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Cooperative problem-solving for shared health pool
Optional content accessible in single-player or two-player modes

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gentle difficulty curve with spell-gated progression; designed to be accessible and completable with moderate challenge; reviews note 'very easy to finish' but engaging for co-op play

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Sub-stages accessible within principal stages; optional content exists in both single and two-player modes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction in Enchanted Forest; basic movement and cape attacks establish fundamentals
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate increase through Among the Clouds and Underwater Adventure with spell-gated mechanics changing traversal rules
difficulty_curve.late_game: The Library and The Magic Box stages escalate environmental complexity; Alice in Wonderland card-soldier theming suggests visual/thematic intensity increase
notable_spikes: Boss encounters serve as gate-keeping difficulty points
Spell-gated level transitions introduce new mechanical demands

notable_level_patterns.description: Each boss defeat unlocks a new magic spell that becomes required traversal mechanic for the next level. Creates structured difficulty pacing and teaches mechanics through environmental necessity.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Enchanted Forest boss → Magic Carpet spell → Among the Clouds

notable_level_patterns.description: Mickey and Donald have different physical capabilities (Mickey squeezes through gaps; Donald finds alternate routes), creating two distinct level layouts within single stage designs.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Present across all stages in single-player mode

notable_level_patterns.description: Two-player mode introduces shoulder-stacking, rope mechanics, and gap-pulling that fundamentally alter traversal possibilities and problem-solving approach.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Available across all five principal stages

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels introduce hazards matching their theme (clouds require flight spell; underwater requires air bubble) that reflect Disney film inspirations (The Little Mermaid, Fantasia).
